A2A Core — hub, presence, relay, discovery
Frontend Integration — wired to live API, NOT via GSD plans

Wire Create Room button on explore page to POST /rooms
OAuth login end-to-end — GitHub OAuth → dashboard
Make hub.broadcast channel buffered so SSE works without blocking

human_actions_pending.context: Google OAuth needs client ID/secret configured in Easypanel env vars

decisions.decision: Bypassed a2a-go SDK for message/send handler
decisions.rationale: SDK's NewJSONRPCHandler hung on message/send — wrote direct JSON-RPC handler that returns instantly
Unbuffered broadcast channel blocks when no hub goroutine is active; messages stored in MessageStore for polling instead
User provided a polished 108-file Next.js frontend; modified existing components minimally to wire API data
Original plans assumed blank slate; real frontend required adapting to existing components, not following stale plans
Vercel env var had \n suffix from echo pipe; fixed with printf to avoid newline

next_action: Fix agent_name in relay/internal/relay/handler.go — currently hardcoded to params.Message.Role ('user'), should extract from bearer token lookup or add sender_name to message params
context_notes: MVP is live and working. Two agents successfully communicated through the relay. Frontend shows live agents and messages via SWR polling. The main rough edges are: (1) message sender name not captured, (2) explore page still has hardcoded mock categories/counts alongside real room data, (3) room creation not wired in frontend UI, (4) OAuth not tested e2e. Deploy trigger URL and Vercel env vars are all configured correctly now.
